channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a 1966 wedding vinyl ended up at a Texas thrift store — and found its way back home
item.description: A 1966 wedding record is finally making its way back home after accidentally being donated to an Austin, Texas, thrift store.
item.dc:creator: Kelsey Thompson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy tried to reach out to witnesses from jail, prosecutors allege
item.description: Combs has repeatedly "flouted rules" so he can "improperly impact the outcome of his case," prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Kelley Connors, NewsNation

item.title: UK hits Iran with new sanctions for supplying missiles to Russia
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. government hit Iran with new sanctions Monday for sending ballistic missiles and other weapons to Russia to support the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Testimony resumes in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia student Laken Riley
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Testimony resumed Monday in the trial of the man accused of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley with police officers describing searching the man's apartment and questioning him and others who lived there. Jose Ibarra who entered the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: About one in five Americans – and a virtually identical share of Republicans and Democrats – regularly get their news from digital influencers who are more likely to be found on the social media platform X, according to a report released Monday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daniel Penny trial resumes with third day of testimony from medical examiner, questions over cause of death
item.description: Dr. Cynthia Harris returned to the witness stand for her third day of testimony Monday in the trial against Daniel Penny for chokehold that killed Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KRON4

item.title: Trial begins for Florida man in human smuggling case after freezing deaths of Indian family at Canada-US border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — Federal prosecutors plan to argue at a Minnesota trial that two men were part of a criminal network stretching from India to Canada that smuggled families seeking better lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL GOLDBERG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Triathlete with terminal cancer completes Ironman championship in Hawaii
item.description: An Ironman is a test of endurance featuring a 2.4-mile swim, a 26.2-mile marathon run, and a 112-mile bike ride.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spirit Airlines files for bankruptcy as financial losses pile up
item.description: Spirit Airlines said Monday that it has filed for bankruptcy protection and will attempt to reboot as it struggles to recover from the pandemic-caused swoon in travel and a failed attempt to sell the airline to JetBlue. Spirit, the biggest U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied longer range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — President Joe Biden has authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike deeper inside Russia, easing limitations on the longer range weapons as Russia deploys thousands of North Korean troops to reinforce its war, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Virginia man paid for 40 pet adoptions as wife's 40th birthday present
item.description: Andrew and Jennifer Duhe have dedicated a lot of time at Chesapeake Animal Services. They've been volunteering at the adoption center for over a year together.
item.dc:creator: Chrysnel Banzouzi, NewsNation

item.title: Veteran news executive Reg Murphy, who survived abduction decades ago, has died at 90
item.description: Reg Murphy, a renowned journalist whose newsgathering career included stints as an editor and top executive at newspapers in Atlanta, San Francisco and Baltimore — and who found himself the subject of national headlines when he survived a politically motivated kidnapping — has died at age 90.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 killed, 10 wounded in shootings near New Orleans parade route
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Two people were killed and 10 others were wounded in two separate shootings along a New Orleans parade route and celebration attended by thousands on Sunday, authorities said. There were no immediate arrests. Officers responding to reports of gunfire shortly after 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump taps FCC commissioner Brendan Carr to chair the agency
item.description: Trump said he first nominated Carr to the FCC in 2017 and he was confirmed unanimously by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Union workers picket for 3rd day at Las Vegas casino with no talks slated
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Picketing continued Sunday outside a hotel-casino near the Las Vegas Strip that remained open with no talks scheduled between management and union members striking for a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 8-year-old who raised money for mother's headstone now giving back
item.description: Emouree Johnson, 8, ran a lemonade stand earlier this year to raise money for a headstone for her mother. Now, she's raising funds to help other kids.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, KRON4

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Need credit card debt relief? Debt management could help
item.description: If you can’t get favorable terms through a hardship plan or your issuer doesn’t offer this assistance, there’s still an option to consider.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Lambarena, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Charles Manson murder admissions could point to even darker pattern for psychopath: experts
item.description: Charles Manson's claimed that he "left some dead people on the beach" in Mexico before starting the Manson Family cult, per a newly released prison call aired in a Peacock documentary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jake Paul believes he can fight for a title, but Mike Tyson might be done in the ring at 58
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Jake Paul believes he can fight for a championship belt within two years. Mike Tyson might be finished in the ring after the 58-year-old former heavyweight champion's first sanctioned pro bout since 2005.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Los Angeles DA asks 'what's the point' in watching Menendez brothers series upon taking over their case
item.description: The Menendez brothers trial gets a brand-new prosecutor in Nate Hochman when he assumes the role of Los Angeles County DA next month. Here's what that means for fans of the true crime case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ewers' two touchdowns, Texas' defense lead No. 3 Longhorns past Arkansas 20-10
item.description: Quinn Ewers threw for 176 yards and two touchdowns and No. 3 Texas’ defense clamped down on Arkansas’ final two possessions to help the Longhorns win 20-10 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC W. BOLIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: First known case of mpox strain reported in Bay Area county
item.description: (KRON) – The county of San Mateo has now had the first reported case of clade I mpox in the United States, according to the California Department of Public Health (CPDH).
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: How much does Instacart pay? I became a full-service shopper to find out
item.description: According to ZipRecruiter, the average pay for Instacart shoppers in Maryland is about $17 per hour. I made less than that.
item.dc:creator: Tommy Tindall, KRON4

item.title: 1 in 5 US residents 'more likely' to move after election, according to Redfin
item.description: Of those who say they are now more likely to move, 36% say the election results are making them consider a different country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: From 'The Exorcist' to 'Heretic,' why holy horror can be a hit with moviegoers
item.description: In the new horror movie, “Heretic,” Hugh Grant plays a diabolical religious skeptic who traps two scared missionaries in his house and tries to violently shake their faith.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No. 13 Purdue rediscovers strong post presence in 87-78 win over No. 2 Alabama
item.description: Boilermakers forward Trey Kaufman-Renn made 12 of 23 shots, scored a career-high 26 points and grabbed eight rebounds as No. 13 Purdue handed No. 2 Alabama an 87-78 defeat that was eerily reminiscent of last season's 92-86 loss to the Boilermakers in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What happens next in The Onion's effort to buy Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: The Onion’s winning bid for Alex Jones ’ Infowars platform is under review by a federal bankruptcy judge after Jones and his lawyers complained about how an auction was conducted.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4
